# ggaction

ggaction is a JavaScript chart-authoring library built around immutable,
hierarchical actions. This file points to canonical documentation for humans
and language models.

## Start

- Documentation index: ./index.md
- Getting started: ./getting-started.md
- Complete scatterplot tutorial: ./tutorials/scatterplot.md
- Complete action reference: ./reference/actions.md
- Supported features: ./supported-features.md

## Core concepts

- ChartProgram and immutability: ./concepts/chart-program.md
- Semantic and graphical state: ./concepts/semantic-and-graphics.md
- Actions and trace trees: ./concepts/actions-and-trace.md

## API

- Canvas: ./api/canvas.md
- Data: ./api/data.md
- Point marks: ./api/point-mark.md
- Encodings: ./api/encodings.md
- Coordinates: ./api/coordinates.md
- Axes: ./api/axes.md
- Rendering: ./api/rendering.md
- Advanced axis components: ./advanced/axis-components.md
- Action authoring: ./extension/action-authoring.md
- Primitive extension API: ./extension/primitives.md

## Architectural invariants

- Every action accepts one plain option object and returns a new ChartProgram.
- Existing programs and caller-owned inputs are never mutated.
- semanticSpec records chart meaning.
- graphicSpec stores fully materialized backend-neutral graphics.
- There is no automatic semantic-to-graphic compiler.
- Domain actions explicitly invoke every required graphical materialization.
- Renderers read graphicSpec only; they do not infer from semanticSpec.
- Nested wrapped actions form the hierarchical trace.
- Positional encodings create and attach coordinates; guide actions only read them.
- encodeRadius is fixed circle appearance, not a Polar radial encoding.

## Current scope

The implemented end-to-end chart is a Cartesian point scatterplot with
quantitative x/y, nominal color, fixed radius, complete axes, browser Canvas,
and PNG output. Do not assume roadmap features are implemented.
